channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study suggests EPA in fish oil may impair brain repair after repeated head injuries
item.description: A new study from the Medical University of South Carolina challenges the widely held belief that fish oil supplements are universally beneficial for brain health. Researchers found that eicosapentaeno...

item.title: Annavaasal Dusk Meal Mission Reaches 25 Lakh People After 1000 Days
item.description: The Annavaasal Dusk Meal Mission, a community-driven initiative in Tamil Nadu, has completed 1000 days of uninterrupted service, feeding over 25 lakh people in total. Launched to ensure no one sleeps ...

item.title: Factors Affecting Car Insurance Premiums in South Africa
item.description: Two motorists can own the same make and model of car and still end up paying very different insurance premiums. This is because insurers consider more than just the vehicle; they also assess the indiv...

item.title: Security concerns raised after attempted shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner ahead of King Charles' US visit
item.description: The attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner has triggered serious concerns over security, particularly in relation to King Charles and Queen Camilla's upcoming state visit to the ...

item.title: Veterans of Afghanistan and Gulf War Seek Recognition as Official War Veterans by Parliament
item.description: Veterans of the Afghanistan and Gulf Wars are expressing hope that the Canadian Parliament will finally recognize them as official ‘war veterans’. After decades of advocacy, these veterans believe tha...

item.title: Marka-i-Haq monument to be completed at Kutchery Chowk before May anniversary
item.description: A new monument titled Marka-i-Haq is currently under construction at Baggi Park, located at Kutchery Chowk in Rawalpindi, just opposite Fatima Jinnah Women University. The project is being executed by...

item.title: Linux 7.1 Removes Legacy ISDN and Obsolete Network Drivers
item.description: The Linux kernel has recently undergone significant cleanup with the removal of the old ISDN subsystem and several outdated network drivers, primarily related to PCMCIA-era network adapters. Linus Tor...

item.title: California Man Identified as Suspect in White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description: Authorities have identified Cole Allen, a 31-year-old computer scientist from Torrance, California, as the suspect in a shooting incident that occurred during the White House Correspondents' Dinner in...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi Condemns Post-Poll Killing of Congress Worker by TMC Thugs
item.description: Rahul Gandhi has strongly condemned the post-poll killing of Congress worker Debdeep Chatterjee in West Bengal, accusing the ruling All India Trinamool Congress (TMC) of creating an atmosphere of 'thu...

item.title: Trump Cancels Iran Talks, Claims Iran Quickly Offered Better Terms
item.description: On April 25, 2026, President Donald Trump canceled in-person ceasefire talks between U.S. envoys and Iranian officials, claiming that Iran presented much better terms immediately after the talks were ...

item.title: Chornobyl Liquidators Revisit Site on 40th Anniversary of Nuclear Disaster
item.description: Forty years after the catastrophic explosion at the Chornobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, a group of former workers known as 'liquidators' returned to the site to commemorate their efforts ...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi Criticises Mamata Banerjee Over Alleged Corruption Amid Bengal Polls
item.description: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi intensified his criticism of West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee during an election rally in Bengal. He questioned why central agencies have investigated him relentlessly but have ...

item.title: KQ Flight Attendant Reflects on Personal Grief After Meeting Terminally Ill Passenger
item.description: Dorothy Were Atemo, a Kenya Airways flight attendant, experienced an emotional encounter that brought back painful memories of her mother's battle with breast cancer. In 2025, during a routine flight,...

item.title: Head-on crash on R23 in Mpumalanga claims four lives and leaves several injured
item.description: Four women lost their lives following a head-on collision between a sedan and a truck on the R23 route between Volksrust and Perdekop in Mpumalanga on Friday evening. According to provincial authoriti...

item.title: TV Patrol Regional Updates for April 26, 2026
item.description: On April 26, 2026, ABS-CBN's TV Patrol Regional aired its latest regional news update, delivering important headlines and updates from various regions of the Philippines. The segment provided key info...

item.title: Western Cape Court Orders Sale of Property Over Unpaid Nedbank Loan
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has ruled in favour of Nedbank Ltd, instructing a Cape Town homeowner, Mandla Stephans, to pay over R1.6 million following his default on a home loan. The dispute stems fro...

item.title: Australia establishes taskforce to address AI-related bioweapons threats
item.description: The Albanese government has launched a high-level national security taskforce to confront the growing threat of AI-driven bioweapons. Experts warn that Australia is vulnerable to catastrophic risks du...

item.title: Albanese and World Leaders React to Donald Trump’s Assassination Attempt at White House Dinner
item.description: In the wake of a failed assassination attempt on US President Donald Trump during a White House Correspondents' Dinner, Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has expressed relief over the safety ...

item.title: FIFA Requests Documentation Following Suspension of FKF President Hussein Mohammed
item.description: FIFA has released a statement regarding the recent suspension of Hussein Mohammed, the president of the Football Kenya Federation (FKF). The FKF National Executive Committee (NEC) suspended Hussein ov...

item.title: Global leaders condemn White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
item.description: Late on Saturday, the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, DC, was disrupted when a man opened fire at the hotel where the event was being held. The shooter, identified as 31-...
